acp.agents.health.description: Get health status for all monitored agents.
acp.agents.health.name: acp_agents_health

acp.agents.list.description:
List available ACP agents and their configuration status.

Returns information about which agents are available and properly configured.
acp.agents.list.name: acp_list_agents

acp.agents.manage.description: Remove a dynamically registered agent (admin only).
acp.agents.manage.name: acp_deregister_agent

acp.agents.manage.description: Update a dynamically registered agent (admin only).
acp.agents.manage.name: acp_update_agent

acp.agents.register.description: Register a new agent type dynamically (admin only).
acp.agents.register.name: acp_register_agent

acp.health.description:
ACP dependency chain health check.

Validates the full stack: runner binary → downstream agent availability → API keys.
Returns structured diagnostics for debugging ACP setup issues.
acp.health.name: acp_health

acp.sessions.manage.description:
Create a new ACP session.

Optionally specify a session name, agent type, tags, and MCP server configs.
acp.sessions.manage.name: acp_session_new

acp.sessions.manage.description: Fork an ACP session from a specific message index.
acp.sessions.manage.name: acp_session_fork

acp.sessions.read.description: List ACP sessions for the authenticated user.
acp.sessions.read.name: acp_list_sessions

acp.sessions.read.description: Query artifacts emitted in ACP session messages.
acp.sessions.read.name: acp_session_artifacts

acp.sessions.read.description: Return ACP audit trail for a session.
acp.sessions.read.name: acp_session_audit

acp.sessions.read.description: Get detailed information about an ACP session.
acp.sessions.read.name: acp_session_detail

acp.sessions.read.description: Return normalized, non-sensitive diagnostics for an ACP session.
acp.sessions.read.name: acp_session_diagnostics

acp.sessions.read.description: Get token usage for an ACP session.
acp.sessions.read.name: acp_session_usage

acp.setup_guide.description:
Return agent-specific setup instructions.

Checks current system state and returns actionable steps to get ACP working.
acp.setup_guide.name: acp_setup_guide

agent_orchestration.projects.manage.description: Delete a project and all associated tasks/runs.
agent_orchestration.projects.manage.name: delete_project

agent_orchestration.projects.read.description: List projects for the current user, optionally filtered by workspace.
agent_orchestration.projects.read.name: list_projects

agent_orchestration.tasks.manage.description: Create a new task in a project.
agent_orchestration.tasks.manage.name: create_task

agent_orchestration.tasks.manage.description:
Submit a review result for a task.

Approved -> complete. Rejected -> back to in_progress or triage (after max attempts).
agent_orchestration.tasks.manage.name: submit_review

agent_orchestration.tasks.manage.description:
Dispatch a task run to an ACP agent.

CWD resolution: explicit cwd > workspace root_path > "."
Workspace MCP servers and env_vars are merged into the session.
agent_orchestration.tasks.manage.name: dispatch_run

agent_orchestration.tasks.read.description: List tasks in a project with optional status filter.
agent_orchestration.tasks.read.name: list_tasks

agent_orchestration.tasks.read.description: Get task detail including run history.
agent_orchestration.tasks.read.name: get_task

agent_orchestration.workspaces.manage.description: Scan a directory tree to discover candidate workspaces.
agent_orchestration.workspaces.manage.name: discover_workspaces

agent_orchestration.workspaces.manage.description: Refresh health status for all workspaces of the current user.
agent_orchestration.workspaces.manage.name: refresh_all_workspace_health

agent_orchestration.workspaces.manage.description: Delete a workspace. Projects are unbound (SET NULL), not deleted.
agent_orchestration.workspaces.manage.name: delete_workspace

agent_orchestration.workspaces.manage.description: Add an MCP server configuration to a workspace.
agent_orchestration.workspaces.manage.name: create_workspace_mcp_server

agent_orchestration.workspaces.manage.description: Remove an MCP server from a workspace.
agent_orchestration.workspaces.manage.name: delete_workspace_mcp_server

agent_orchestration.workspaces.read.description: List all workspaces for the current user.
agent_orchestration.workspaces.read.name: list_workspaces

agent_orchestration.workspaces.read.description: Get a workspace with children and MCP servers.
agent_orchestration.workspaces.read.name: get_workspace

agent_orchestration.workspaces.read.description: On-demand health check for a single workspace.
agent_orchestration.workspaces.read.name: check_workspace_health

agent_orchestration.workspaces.read.description: List MCP servers configured for a workspace.
agent_orchestration.workspaces.read.name: list_workspace_mcp_servers

any.description: Returns available chat slash commands with their descriptions. When permission enforcement is enabled, commands requiring a permission are filtered by the current user's privileges in multi-user mode.
any.name: list_chat_commands

any.description: Generates an assistant response using the configured LLM provider. Supports OpenAI-compatible request schema, optional SSE streaming via `stream=true`, character/world book context, and chat dictionaries. Non-stream responses include `tldw_conversation_id` for client state. Authentication headers are validated via dependencies (AuthNZ); the declared header parameters are included for OpenAPI documentation clarity.
any.name: create_chat_completion
list_chat_conversations
any.summary: List/search conversations with filters and ranking

any.description:
Retrieve all unique citations from a conversation's messages.

    This aggregates all retrieved documents from RAG context across
    all messages, useful for generating a citation bibliography
    or export.
any.name: get_conversation_citations
any.summary: Get all citations from a conversation

any.description:
Retrieve messages from a conversation with their RAG context attached.

    This is optimized for the Knowledge QA page to load conversation history
    with full citation data for each message.
any.name: get_messages_with_rag_context
any.summary: Get conversation messages with RAG context

any.name: list_conversation_share_links
any.summary: List tokenized share links for a conversation

any.name: create_conversation_share_link
any.summary: Create a tokenized share link for a conversation

any.description: Validates a chat dictionary JSON payload and returns a structured report including errors, warnings, and basic entry statistics.
any.name: validate_chat_dictionary

any.description: Persist a snippet from a conversation into Notes (and optional Flashcard).
any.name: save_chat_knowledge
any.summary: Save a chat snippet to Notes/Flashcards with backlinks

any.description: Retrieve the RAG context stored with a message, including citations and search settings.
any.name: get_rag_context
any.summary: Get RAG context for a message

any.description:
Store RAG search results, citations, and settings with a message.

    This endpoint allows the frontend to persist RAG context after a search
    is performed, enabling citation persistence for Knowledge QA conversations.

    The RAG context includes:
    - Search query and mode used
    - Retrieved documents with scores and excerpts
    - Generated answer (if any)
    - Citation metadata
    - Settings snapshot for reproducibility
any.name: persist_rag_context
any.summary: Persist RAG context with a message

any.name: list_chat_conversations
any.summary: List/search conversations with filters and ranking [alias]

any.name: list_conversation_share_links
any.summary: List tokenized share links for a conversation [alias]

any.name: create_conversation_share_link
any.summary: Create a tokenized share link for a conversation [alias]

any.description:
Ingest and process web content from various scraping strategies.

Supports:
  - individual: each URL scraped independently
  - sitemap:   treat the first URL as a sitemap and scrape it
  - url_level: scrape pages up to a path depth from the base URL
  - recursive: crawl links up to max_depth / max_pages
any.name: ingest_web_content

any.description:
The unified RAG search endpoint with ALL features accessible via parameters.

    **Key Features:**
    - No configuration files needed
    - Every feature is a direct parameter
    - Mix and match any features
    - Transparent execution

    **Available Features:**
    - Query expansion (acronym, synonym, domain, entity)
    - Semantic caching with adaptive thresholds
    - Multi-database search (media, notes, characters, chats)
    - Security filtering and PII detection
    - Citation generation (APA, MLA, Chicago, Harvard)
    - Answer generation from context
    - Document reranking (FlashRank, Cross-Encoder, Hybrid)
    - Table processing and extraction
    - Enhanced chunking with parent context
    - User feedback collection
    - Performance monitoring and observability
    - Batch processing support
    - Resilience features (retries, circuit breakers)

    Simply set any feature parameter to enable it. All parameters are optional
    except the query itself.
any.name: unified_search_endpoint

any.description: Execute a read-only SQL query through Text2SQL policy guardrails.
any.name: query_text2sql

rag.search.description:
The unified RAG search endpoint with ALL features accessible via parameters.

    **Key Features:**
    - No configuration files needed
    - Every feature is a direct parameter
    - Mix and match any features
    - Transparent execution

    **Available Features:**
    - Query expansion (acronym, synonym, domain, entity)
    - Semantic caching with adaptive thresholds
    - Multi-database search (media, notes, characters, chats)
    - Security filtering and PII detection
    - Citation generation (APA, MLA, Chicago, Harvard)
    - Answer generation from context
    - Document reranking (FlashRank, Cross-Encoder, Hybrid)
    - Table processing and extraction
    - Enhanced chunking with parent context
    - User feedback collection
    - Performance monitoring and observability
    - Batch processing support
    - Resilience features (retries, circuit breakers)

    Simply set any feature parameter to enable it. All parameters are optional
    except the query itself.
rag.search.name: unified_search_endpoint

scheduler.workflows.admin_rescan.description:
Force a one-shot rescan of all users' schedules.

Requires the ``workflows.admin`` permission (via
:data:`tldw_Server_API.app.core.AuthNZ.permissions.WORKFLOWS_ADMIN`) or an
admin principal. Returns the number of registered APScheduler jobs after
rescan.
scheduler.workflows.admin_rescan.name: admin_rescan

scheduler.workflows.dry_run.description:
Validate cron/timezone and return next run time and echo inputs.

Notes:
- Timezone must be an IANA tz name (e.g., 'UTC', 'America/New_York').
  See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones
- Does not persist a schedule or submit a run.
scheduler.workflows.dry_run.name: dry_run_schedule

text2sql.query.description: Execute a read-only SQL query through Text2SQL policy guardrails.
text2sql.query.name: query_text2sql

workflows.description: Delete expired ephemeral collections according to TTL registry.
workflows.name: cleanup_ephemeral_collections
